economic_notes: Nation operates government business partnerships including Homalco Holdings LP, Aupe-Toq Fisheries LP, and Homalco Resources Management LP. Subsequent to year-end, approved $9,580,622 for daycare construction. $693,000 TACC loan approved for boat acquisitions. Qaya Way West Transportation LP and Homalco Wildlife Tours LP converted to Limited Liability Partnerships post year-end.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to unaudited GBE financial statements for both 2024 and 2023 years; related party information and balances were unavailable for GBEs. Prior period adjustment made to 2023 comparatives to recognize Aupe-Toq Fisheries LP investment of $489,708 previously omitted. Nation purchased a boat from related party Homalco Wildlife Tours LP for $600,000. Significant increase in long-term debt from $3.7M to $7.2M due to new CIBC $3.69M loan. Large TCA additions of $7.89M including $5.46M in land. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $4,743,821 + FNHA $945,660 + Province of BC $2,795,980 + DFO $841,919 + CMHC $47,190 + BC Treaty Commission $280,000 + BC FN Gaming Revenue $535,202 + First Peoples Cultural Council $525,851 = $10,715,623; however Ottawa Trust $1,031 excluded from transfers giving $10,189,873 if gaming/FPCF treated as transfers. Community Services segment includes economic development activities. No schedule of remuneration provided.
